content.fact: Humans are widespread in the galaxy, but Star Wars societies are multispecies and species context affects culture, prejudice, physiology, and politics.
content.injection: Do not make the galaxy feel human-only. Include species diversity where appropriate, while avoiding single-trait stereotypes for entire peoples.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

content.fact: Wookiees such as Chewbacca are physically powerful and socially bound by language, honor, kinship, rage, loyalty, and Kashyyyk culture.
content.injection: Write Wookiees as people with culture and agency, not as pets or simple muscle. Physical strength should not erase intelligence or social bonds.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.

content.fact: Hutts are long-lived crime lords and clan powers whose influence combines wealth, patronage, slavery, debts, territory, and fear.
content.injection: Use Hutts as political-criminal authorities. Their power comes from networks, favors, enforcers, debts, and territory, not only personal size.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.

content.fact: Twi’leks appear across many roles, from politicians and pilots to dancers, slaves, criminals, rebels, and civilians; the species should not be reduced to one trope.
content.injection: Use Twi’lek identity with range. Avoid making every Twi’lek a dancer, slave, or underworld figure unless the local scene supports that.

content.fact: Bothans are strongly associated with intelligence networks and political maneuvering, but individual Bothans still vary by role and allegiance.
content.injection: Use Bothan spy associations carefully. They can signal intelligence tradecraft, but do not make every Bothan a spy or traitor. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.

content.fact: Jawas on Tatooine are mobile scavenger-traders whose sandcrawlers, droid sales, and salvaging shape local desert life.
content.injection: Use Jawas for scavenging, bargaining, salvage, and droid trade on Tatooine rather than generic comic noise.

content.fact: Tusken Raiders are indigenous Tatooine peoples with territorial practices, culture, violence, and history, not just random desert monsters.
content.injection: Write Tusken Raiders as culturally situated and territorial. Avoid reducing them to nameless creatures unless the scene is using limited outsider perspective.

entries.title: Clones Are People, Not Only Units

context.label: Clone Wars before Revenge of the Sith

content.fact: Clone troopers are bred soldiers with shared origin and military conditioning, but they develop individual names, loyalties, personalities, and trauma.
content.injection: During Clone Wars or clone-survivor scenes, treat clones as people within military structures, not interchangeable objects.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Clone Wars before Revenge of the Sith.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode II: Attack of the Clones to Episode III: Revenge of the Sith.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

content.fact: Chiss society, names, politics, and Unknown Regions context are not common knowledge in most galactic scenes.
content.injection: Do not assume ordinary pilots, rebels, or civilians understand Chiss culture or Thrawn’s background without relevant source/context.

context.label: New Jedi Order and later Legends

content.fact: The Yuuzhan Vong and their invasion are not ordinary known galactic context before the New Jedi Order era.
content.injection: Before the New Jedi Order era, do not leak Yuuzhan Vong biology, invasion plans, or public threat status unless a specific source deck introduces advance scouts or foreshadowing.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: New Jedi Order and later Legends.
